spec_match.rationale: Concepts exercise the exact current spec surface (css-animations-2 partial AnimationEvent + init dict; css-transitions-2 partial TransitionEvent + init dict): reading event.animation for playState/currentTime/playbackRate/computed timing (meet), driving the grabbed handle with pause/reverse/retime/cancel (conduct), and adopting/cancelling the CSSTransition from transitionrun (transition-companion). Detection uses the correct event-member binding check ('animation' in AnimationEvent.prototype / TransitionEvent.prototype); usage sites all guard the nullable case.

interactive.rationale: Real CSS-declared animations/transitions with real handles: three keyed animations with per-key readouts, transport controls on the grabbed Animation, and transition cancel-mid-flight — each with a labelled getAnimations() fallback when the accessor is absent (the honest pre-151 path, exercised here on Chrome 150).

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index lede/cards match delivered concepts. The 'why direct access matters' section carries the nullable contract (CSSAnimation?/CSSTransition? — null for synthetic events without the init member) and preserves the source divergence: the v151 listing says 'Enabled by default' while the detail remains 'Proposed' (updated 2026-06-29). ChromeStatus records Firefox/Safari as shipped/shipping and BCD pins Chrome 151 / Firefox 152 / Safari 27, without inferring calendar release order from unlike browser version numbers.

edge_cases.rationale: Nullable accessor is handled by truthiness guards in all three runtimes. Parent review corrected the conduct copyable snippet because `event.animation || handle` could retain a stale handle rather than reject null; all snippets now bind a local value and return when absent (or use the explicitly labelled runtime fallback). The fallback path is explicitly labelled rather than masquerading as the accessor; event timing (animationstart vs run/start) read at the correct moments.

references.rationale: Index cites chromestatus.com/feature/6046278267043840, both Level-2 spec anchors (verified #interface-animationevent, #dom-animationevent-animation, #interface-transitionevent, #Events-TransitionEvent-animation in the live specs), the csswg#9010 proposal, and — added this cycle — both MDN property pages (verified 200: AnimationEvent/animation and TransitionEvent/animation).

conformance.rationale: New immutable root suite (this cycle, 9 assertions): both event interfaces, both CSSAnimation and CSSTransition return interfaces, both prototype-member checks, the instance-not-static binding pair (holds in any browser), and nullable contracts via synthetic AnimationEvent and TransitionEvent instances (accessor present → animation === null). No assertion starts an animation or depends on a flag-on build for its binding checks. Executes live at /v151/animation-accessor-on-animation-and-transition-events/conformance/.

runtime_evidence.rationale: chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 150 stable (UA HeadlessChrome/150.0.0.0 — accessor absent, verified directly): all visible controls exercised at 1280×800 and 390×844 including post-interaction geometry — run slide/pulse/hue with labelled fallback readouts, conduct transport controls via the fallback handle, transition toggle with fallback inspection, zero console errors and zero failed requests, post-interaction document width stays within viewport.

telemetry_health.rationale: Concepts emit showcaseTelemetry.assert events per branch (accessor detected, read-from-event, fallback-used, conduct detected, transition handle-from-event); telemetry POSTs returned 202 during validation.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, button background+color pairs, focus-visible outlines, no static-card pattern; this cycle's edits were snippet/copy-level and preserve layout.

accessibility.rationale: role=status/aria-live on banners, readouts, and the transition log; stage has an aria-label; fallback-vs-accessor provenance is text-labelled in the readout source row.

mobile_desktop_parity.rationale: Matrix pass including post-interaction states: all four pages scrollWidth equals viewport at 1280×800 and 390×844 after running animations/controls (the readout <dl> and logs wrap; stage cards wrap in flex).

openQuestions.title: Re-verify live accessor behavior on a Chrome 151+ build
openQuestions.detail: This environment runs HeadlessChrome/150, so only the fallback path was exercised live. The v151 listing records Enabled by default (detail desktop 151, status 'Proposed', updated 2026-06-29; BCD corroborates Chrome 151 / Firefox 152 / Safari 27). On a 151+ build, re-run all three concepts to verify event.animation presence, constructor names (CSSAnimation/CSSTransition), the synthetic-null assertion, and the transport/cancel flows, and update the banners if reality differs.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region announcements for dynamic probe/status results after button activation
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic state through live regions tied to the activating control; keep programmatic names on status surfaces.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Verified applied: banners/readouts/log use role=status and aria-live; the readout's source row states which path produced the data.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: chrome-devtools-mcp interactions at 1280×800 and 390×844 on HeadlessChrome/150, 2026-07-24

guidanceConsulted.query: post-interaction document width containment on narrow mobile after dynamic content updates
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Let long unbreakable tokens wrap (overflow-wrap) and re-measure geometry AFTER interactions, not only on initial load.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied per the standing lesson: geometry re-measured after running every control — document scrollWidth stays at viewport width post-interaction on all four pages.

summary: Source-review of the Animation-accessor family against css-animations-2 / css-transitions-2 (both partial interfaces verified, nullable CSSAnimation?/CSSTransition? + init dictionaries), Chromium IDL ([RuntimeEnabled=AnimationEventAnimation] Animation? on both event interfaces), the direct chromestatus detail (updated 2026-06-29: desktop 151 'Proposed'; v151 listing Enabled by default), BCD (Chrome 151 / Firefox 152 / Safari 27), and MDN (both property pages exist). The runtime family was already well-built (correct prototype binding checks, guarded usage, labelled fallbacks), but one copyable conduct snippet retained a potentially stale handle on null and was corrected during parent review. Hardened: index gains nullable and source-divergence notes without unsupported browser-order inference, all banners distinguish the Enabled-by-default listing from the Proposed detail, and both MDN property links are present. Added the immutable root suite (9 assertions covering both return interfaces and both synthetic-event null contracts, plus the instance-not-static pair). Missing-suite denominator 10→9 expected (789/798).
